characters.name: Selma Ahrens
characters.description: Selma AhrensOccupationChild Protective Services Case WorkerBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceTruthsLast AppearanceAlpha and OmegaPortrayed byBarbara PhilippSelma Ahrensis a case worker from Child Protective Services (CPS) in1986. She interacts mostly withInes KahnwaldatWindenHospitalregarding the welfare ofMikkel. Ines reveals toSelma Ahrensthat she previously had a son who died shortly after he was born.[1]

In his youth he is a student atWinden Comprehensive School, but in 2019 he is roped into the world of time travel by his own older self. From that point on, he followsEva(an older Martha Nielsen) and is a member of her time travel cultErit Lux.

characters.name: Clausen
characters.description: 20201986Wilhelm ClausenDate of Birth1972Date of DeathJune 27, 2020 (Apocalypse)StatusDeceased  (Apocalypse)ResidenceMarburg(former)WindenFamilyFamilyAleksander Köhler(Brother)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceBeginnings and EndingsLast AppearanceEndings and BeginningsPortrayed bySylvester GrothInspectorWilhelm[1]Clausenis the stern head of the federal task force investigating the disappearances inWinden, as well as the mystery relating to his late brother, Aleksander Köhler. After his parents’ seperation, he assumed the name of his mother: “Clausen” which also helped to keep him untraceable form. He was last seen in season 2. Winden Clausen used to live in Marburg with his brother and parents. In 1986, some time before November 11th, his brother Aleksander was killed byBoris Niewald, who went on to assume his identity. Clausen, assuming his mother’s familial name, would spend a long period of time over the next 33 years searching for his brother across Germany, he soon received a letter fromThe Unknown,[2]who told him he would be able to find answers concerning his brother in Winden. Due to this, he volunteered for the Winden Task Force.

From an early age, Charlotte was deeply affected by never knowing who her parents were, giving her a somewhat flat emotional affect.

Tannhaus(adoptive grandfather)Aleksander Tiedemann†(great-grandfather)Regina Tiedemann†(great-grandmother/first cousin, once removed)Claudia Tiedemann†(great-great-grandmother/grandaunt)Egon Tiedemann†(great-grandfather/great-great-great-grandfather)Doris Tiedemann†(great-great-great-grandmother)Agnes Nielsen(aunt/great-great-great-great-grandaunt)Tronte Nielsen(cousin/great-great-great-granduncle)Ulrich Nielsen(first cousin, once removed/great-great-granduncle)Mads Nielsen†(first cousin, once removed/great-great-granduncle)Magnus Nielsen(first cousin, twice removed)Martha Nielsen†(first cousin, twice removed)Mikkel Nielsen†(first cousin, twice removed/great-granduncle)Jonas Kahnwald(first cousin, thrice removed/granduncle)Hannah Kahnwald†(great-grandmother) Charlotte Doppleris chief of theWinden Policein 2019. She is married toPeter Dopplerwith whom she has two daughters,FranziskaandElisabeth. From an early age, Charlotte was deeply affected by never knowing who her parents were, giving her a somewhat flat emotional affect.

characters.name: Claudia Doppler
characters.description: Claudia DopplerDate of BirthAugust 14, 1942StatusAliveResidenceWinden,Original WorldFamilyFamilyEgon Tiedemann(father)Doris Tiedemann(mother)Bernd Doppler(husband)Regina Doppler(daughter)Physical DescriptionHairBrownEyesHeterochromia, brown (left) & blue (right)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceThe ParadiseLast AppearanceThe ParadisePortrayed byJulika Jenkins Doris Tiedemann(mother)Bernd Doppler(husband)Regina Doppler(daughter) Claudia Doppler(néeTiedemann) is a resident ofWindenin theOriginal World, and the second wife ofBernd Doppler. She lives with her husband and daughter,Regina, in a beautiful old house on the edge of the woods.[1]

characters.name: Greta Doppler
characters.description: Greta DopplerDate of Birth1917StatusDeceased (in 1972)ResidenceWindenOccupationHousewifeFamilyFamilyBernd Doppler(Husband)Anatol Veliev(Rapist)Helge Doppler(Son)Ulla Schmidt(Daughter- In Law)Peter Doppler(Grandson)Charlotte Doppler(Granddaughter- In Law/Great-Great-Granddaughter)Franziska Doppler(Great-Granddaughter)Elisabeth Doppler(Great-Granddaughter)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceAs You Sow, so You Shall ReapPortrayed byCordelia WegeGreta Doppleris the mother ofHelge Dopplerand the wife ofBernd Doppler. Anatol Veliev(Rapist)Helge Doppler(Son)Ulla Schmidt(Daughter- In Law)Peter Doppler(Grandson)Charlotte Doppler(Granddaughter- In Law/Great-Great-Granddaughter)Franziska Doppler(Great-Granddaughter)Elisabeth Doppler(Great-Granddaughter) Greta is a stern mother to Helge; when he arrives home muddy one day in 1953, she forces him to disrobe in the entrance hall of the grandDoppler residence. After Helge goes missing, she implies toNoahthat Bernd is not Helge's father, but rather Helge is the product of rape. She also confesses she had once hoped that God would have taken Helge away.[1]

characters.name: Peter Doppler
characters.description: AdultYoungPeter DopplerDate of Birth1970[1]Date of DeathSeptember 25, 2020[2](Aged around 50)StatusDeceased(stabbed in throat by a marauder)Alive(Original World)ResidenceWindenOccupationTherapistFamilyFamilyHelge Doppler†(father)Ulla Schmidt†(mother)Greta Doppler†(grandmother)Anatol Veliev(biological grandfather)Bernd Doppler†(step-grandfather)Charlotte Doppler(wife/granddaughter)Franziska Doppler(daughter)Elisabeth Doppler(daughter/mother-in-law)Hanno Tauber†(son-in-law/father-in-law)Regina Doppler†(step-aunt)Bernadette Wöller†(ex-lover)Physical DescriptionHairBrownEyesBlueBehind the ScenesPortrayed byStephan Kampwirth(adult)Pablo Striebeck (young) Alive(Original World) Ulla Schmidt†(mother)Greta Doppler†(grandmother)Anatol Veliev(biological grandfather)Bernd Doppler†(step-grandfather)Charlotte Doppler(wife/granddaughter)Franziska Doppler(daughter)Elisabeth Doppler(daughter/mother-in-law)Hanno Tauber†(son-in-law/father-in-law)Regina Doppler†(step-aunt)Bernadette Wöller†(ex-lover) Peter is reserved, and suffers silently, not showing his emotions, even with family members. He has many internal conflicts. His sexual desires conflict with his responsibilities to his family and his marriage. He also considers confessing to Charlotte something (presumably that the dead boy found in the woods is indeed Mads Nielsen), but he always falls short of doing it.
characters.personality_section: Peter is reserved, and suffers silently, not showing his emotions, even with family members. He has many internal conflicts. characters.name: Martin Döhring
characters.description: Martin DöhringResidenceWindenOccupationPolice Chief (1986)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceCrossroadsLast AppearanceThe SurvivorsPortrayed byFranz Hartwig Martin Döhringis theWindenChief ofPolicein1986. He asksEgonto interview theplantpersonnel for routes and times based on shift schedules for the nightMadsdisappeared. When Egon hesitates because his is re-consideringUlrich'sinvolvement since the rape charge,Martinscolds him saying: Egon has only 3 months remaining until retirement. He can do whatever he wants then, but until thenMartinsays he will do what he wants.[1]

characters.name: Erna
characters.description: 19211904Erna JungResidenceWindenOccupationTavern & Inn KeeperPhysical DescriptionHairDark BrownBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceThe TravelersLast AppearanceBetween the TimePortrayed byTilla Kratochwil(Adult),Anja Willutzki(Young)Erna Jungis aWindenTavern and Inn Keeper in1921. She is known to take in strays or people in need of food and shelter on a temporary basis. She took the injuredJonasin when he appeared in1921after some local farmers came across him in a wheat field and assumed he was a returningPOW. Also youngErnaappears as midwife duringSilja'schildbirth.

characters.name: Yasin Friese
characters.description: Yasin FrieseDate of Birth2010[1]Date of DeathNovember 9, 1953(aged 9)[2]StatusDead(unintentionally killed byNoahandHelge Dopplervia the time travelchair)ResidenceWindenOccupationStudentPhysical DescriptionHeight1.35 m[1]HairBrownEyesBrownBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceDouble LivesLast AppearanceAs You Sow, so You Shall Reap(corpse)Portrayed byVico MückeYasin Frieseis a young deaf child in 2019 and the "boyfriend" ofElisabeth Doppler. He andElisabethare seen chatting with one another after school on November 6, 2019, the day of Elisabeth's (temporary) disappearance. Yasin's mother offers to drive Elisabeth home, but Elisabeth declines, expectingher motherat any minute.[3] Stranger danger

characters.name: Gretchen
characters.description: Claudia Tiedemann's dog GretchenGretchenDate of Birth1950StatusCeased to exist[1]OccupationDogFamilyFamilyClaudia Tiedemann(owner)(formerly)Regina Tiedemann†(owner)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceAs You Sow, so You Shall ReapLast AppearanceBetween the TimeGretchenbegan life a poodle belonging to the eleven-year-oldClaudia Tiedemannin 1953. Regina Tiedemann†(owner) On November 10, she goes missing in the woods while Claudia andTronte Nielsen, a newcomer toWindenand Claudia's new housemate, are walking together. Also present on the walk isHelge Doppler, a couple years younger than Claudia and also tutored by her. He becomes jealous of the attention Claudia shows Tronte and throws a stick into thecave, which Gretchen chases.[2]The poodle never returns to the outside world.

characters.name: Edda Heimann
characters.description: Edda HeimannResidenceWindenOccupationPathologistBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceLiesLast AppearanceCrossroadsPortrayed byAnna KönigEdda Heimannis a Pathologist in2019. On November 5th, 2019, she examines theboy's bodyfound by the search party and tellsCharlotteof her findings. The burns around his eyes are strange as if melted. His ear canals where destroyed, the inner ear damaged, and the eardrums burst. The otoconia grains inside his ears (which help keep our balance) are not where they should be. This could be caused by pressure from a nosedive or intense rotation such as a centrifuge.[1] On November 6th, 2019,Eddaalso examines one of thedead birdsand tellsCharlottethat she is no onithologists but the white speckles on the feathers are not typical of the species.[2]
